formations_blocks: Czechia passing and moving well in possession
South Africa defensive shape tightening into an immediate block when they do win the ball
South Africa having only one man back at the decisive early attack

Maseko and Appollis combining on South Africa's first meaningful attack
phases.summary: Czechia began on the front foot, moving the ball crisply and creating an early chance before capitalising from a set-piece sequence for the opening goal. South Africa’s shape looked vulnerable in transition and at restarts, though they later started to find wider areas and briefly threatened through Maseko, Appollis and a long-range effort.

formations_blocks: South Africa in sustained possession/attacking phase
Czechia appearing to sit back and protect their lead
Czechia intermittently pushing the pace rather than holding a fixed deep block
adjustments: South Africa shifting the ball right to build attacks
South Africa continuing to probe and push Czechia back
Czechia seemingly becoming more conservative after scoring, with a possible reluctance to lose their lead
key_matchups: Modiba to Adams on the right-side progression
Mudau delivering the cross toward Rayners
Rayners challenging for the cross against the Czechia defence
Appollis playing a straight pass into Rayners
Mbatha arriving to collect the squared ball
phases.summary: South Africa dominate possession through this stretch and repeatedly work the ball toward the right flank, with Adams, Mudau and Appollis helping progress attacks into dangerous areas. Czechia look increasingly focused on protecting their lead and spending more time deeper, though the commentary suggests South Africa’s pressure is also forcing them back. The clearest attacking pattern is SA’s right-sided circulation and crossing into Rayners, but the final ball or support run is just missing to turn pressure into a chance.

The text only confirms the half-time score, with Czechia leading South Africa 1-0. It contains no tactical description of formations, pressing structures, adjustments, or player matchups, so there is no actionable football analysis to extract from this phase.

phases.summary: This phase was defined by both sides creating half-chances through direct, vertical attacks rather than long spells of control. South Africa began to find more success progressing through midfield and into the right-sided channel, while Czechia repeatedly threatened from the left and via set pieces, though their finishing was loose. Substitutions on both sides suggested a search for sharper movement and better occupation of central spaces, but the game remained open and slightly fragmented.

formations_blocks: South Africa attacking with four defenders back on a set piece/free-kick situation
Czechia sitting on a lead before later opening up to attack
Both sides in a disjointed, open late-game phase rather than a clearly structured press/block
adjustments: South Africa repeatedly take free-kicks short rather than crossing immediately into the box
South Africa commit more bodies forward after the equaliser in search of a winner
Czechia begin to push forward late, with Zeleny delivering into the box
key_matchups: Krejci vs Maseko (Krejci fouls him for a booking)
Soucek vs Mokoena (Soucek hauls Mokoena down)
Sulc vs Maseko (Maseko's cross strikes Sulc’s arm)
Kovar vs Mafokeng (Kovar spills a low shot)
phases.summary: This phase is defined by South Africa chasing the game, gradually increasing pressure through set pieces and direct attacks until a contentious handball penalty brings them level. Czechia had earlier chances to kill the match but, after dropping deeper and failing to manage the lead, they were forced into a frantic final stretch where both sides looked ragged and neither controlled the tempo.
